_doc: External integrations for one edition. Every integration is OPTIONAL and individually toggleable, so a community with no webshop (or a different webshop) can run the hub with all of this disabled. The app must treat every integration as off unless explicitly enabled here. Secrets are NEVER in this file - only the non-secret settings. Secret values (API keys, SMTP password) live in Azure Key Vault and are referenced here by their Key Vault secret name.

woocommerce._doc: WooCommerce order pull, used to drive the sponsor pipeline. Set enabled:false for any community that does not use WooCommerce - the sponsor module then works purely from manual CSV import or hand entry, with no API.
woocommerce._ordersWindowNote: ISO date window passed to the WooCommerce REST API as ?after= / ?before= so the pull only returns orders inside [ordersAfter, ordersBefore). Both ends should match THIS edition's webshop sales window. ordersAfter = the day the edition's webshop opened (everything before is a previous-edition order and must not generate tasks for the current edition). ordersBefore = the day of the event (strictly-before semantics per WooCommerce REST - 2027-02-09 means orders up to and including 2027-02-08). For ELDK27: webshop opened 1 May 2026, event starts 9 Feb 2027 - so the window is 2026-05-01 .. 2027-02-08 inclusive. Leave a field blank to disable that end of the window (NOT recommended - legacy orders pollute the task list).
woocommerce._secretsNote: consumerKeySecretName / consumerSecretSecretName are the NAMES of secrets in Azure Key Vault, not the values. Generate a READ-ONLY WooCommerce REST API key (WooCommerce > Settings > Advanced > REST API). The app reads orders only; it never writes to the webshop.
woocommerce._scheduleNote: The daily pull cadence is configured under scheduledJobs.woocommercePull below, not here.
woocommerce._scopeNote: Pulls the WooCommerce orders API only (order, line items, billing company/contact). It does NOT replicate the old PowerShell's WordPress approver-meta enrichment - that was slow and fragile. Add later only if a community specifically needs the announcement-name correlation.

companyManager._doc: Company Manager is a custom WordPress plugin on expertslive.dk that owns sponsor COMPANIES, their CONTACTS, and contact ROLES. The hub reads this as the source of truth for sponsor company/contact/role data (see CONTEXT.md 11g). Set enabled:false for a community without it - the hub then manages sponsor contacts itself.
companyManager._secretsNote: HTTP Basic auth with a WordPress application password. Both values in Azure Key Vault; only the secret names appear here.

roleIds._doc: Company Manager's real role IDs. Role 1 = Signer (a company's default_signer_id). Role 2 = Event Coordinator (event_coordination_default_contact_id). Reminders target role 2, never role 1.

companyManager._orderLinkNote: WooCommerce orders carry this meta key linking the order to a Company Manager company - used by the sponsor order pull to attach orders to the right company. Orders without it are legacy and skipped.

zoho._doc: Zoho integration for the attendee area (CONTEXT.md 9z). Reconciles Zoho Backstage ticket orders against Zoho Bookings Master Class appointments. Optional - set enabled:false for a community not using Zoho. EU data centre.
zoho._idsNote: Backstage portal + event IDs - from the Zoho Backstage admin. Placeholders until confirmed.
zoho._regexNote: How the reconciliation recognises a Master Class booking service and a 2-day ticket class, by name. Same patterns as the source PowerShell script.
zoho._dateNote: Pre-day / Master Class date - the Bookings fetch window is this day 00:00:00-23:59:59.
zoho._secretsNote: OAuth refresh-token flow. All three values in Key Vault; only the secret names appear here. The script's existing credentials are compromised - rotate before use.

email._doc: Outbound email - PIN codes, reminders, summaries. Required for the hub to function (PIN auth needs it). provider selects the mechanism.
email._fromNote: fromEmail must be a sender address verified/authenticated in Brevo (domain or single-sender verification). Brevo will reject mail from an unverified sender.

smtp._doc: Brevo SMTP relay. IMPORTANT: the SMTP username is NOT info@expertslive.dk - it is the Brevo-issued SMTP login (typically like 8xxxxxx@smtp-brevo.com), and the password is a Brevo SMTP key, not the Brevo account password. Both are stored in Azure Key Vault under the secret names above; only the names appear here. host/port/useStartTls are non-secret. Find the SMTP login + key in Brevo: SMTP & API > SMTP.

azureCommunicationServices._doc: Alternative provider, unused while provider is 'smtp'. Kept for portability - another community could switch here.

calendar._doc: Generate .ics calendar invites for hotel confirmations and shifts. Pure generation, no external service - safe to leave on.

scheduledJobs._doc: Timer-driven background jobs. Run by an Azure Functions timer-triggered app (see CONTEXT.md section 11c). cron is a 6-field NCRONTAB expression in UTC. Each job is idempotent - it re-evaluates current state each run and records what it sent, so a missed run is caught next time and nothing is sent twice.

reminderJob._doc: The reminder job WAKES once a day at 06:00 UTC, but it does NOT send everything daily. It applies the per-audience cadences in content config -> reminders: speaker pending-task digests and sponsor overdue chases go WEEKLY (on their configured weekday), milestone reminders fire relative to deadline dates. The daily wake is just so weekly/milestone rules can be evaluated each day; actual sends follow the cadence.

woocommercePull._doc: Once daily at 03:00 UTC. Pulls new completed orders from WooCommerce and expands them into sponsor tasks. Ignored when integrations.woocommerce.enabled is false.

testMode._doc: TESTMODE. When enabled=true, integrations make NO real outbound writes/sends: the Backstage exhibitor sync makes no Zoho calls and coordinator emails go ONLY to testCoordinatorEmail. Exercise the full flow with the test sponsor before going live. Set enabled=false for production.

backstageSync._doc: Backstage exhibitor sync. Derives sponsors from completed WooCommerce orders, checks each against Zoho Backstage, creates missing ones (when the Backstage write API is confirmed) and emails the coordinator. NOTE: the Backstage exhibitor-creation endpoint is NOT yet confirmed - until then the sync records 'would-create' and notifies the coordinator.

backstageExhibitor._doc: Live Zoho Backstage exhibitor API settings. Uses the v3 Add-exhibitor-request endpoint (POST .../exhibitor_requests). Reuses zoho.backstagePortalId and zoho.backstageEventId. The Zoho refresh token must include the OAuth scope zohobackstage.exhibitor.CREATE. defaultBoothCategoryId: get this id from the event's booth categories in Backstage - creation is disabled until it is set.
